sveltejs/kit#15056.body:
### Describe the bug

Recommend `nodejs_compat` flag instead of putting it in the troubleshooting section. Related:

- https://github.com/sveltejs/kit/issues/15029

```
 Svelte error: async_local_storage_unavailable
      The node API `AsyncLocalStorage` is not available, but is required to use async server rendering.
      https://svelte.dev/e/async_local_storage_unavailable
```

Guide users to set the `compatibility_date` to the current date. [Reference](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/compatibility-dates/#setting-compatibility-date)

> When you start your project, you should always set `compatibility_date` to the current date.

```jsonc
{
	"name": "<any-name-you-want>",
	"main": ".svelte-kit/cloudflare/_worker.js",
	"compatibility_date": "2025-01-01", // almost an year has passed
	"assets": {
		"binding": "ASSETS",
		"directory": ".svelte-kit/cloudflare",
	}
}
```

Add a `wrangler.jsonc` example for the Cloudflare Pages section.

This helps adding the `nodejs_compat` flag A LOT easier.

```diff
- assets # reserved for Workers only
+ pages_build_output_dir # required
```

> The name 'ASSETS' is reserved in Pages projects. Please use a different name for your Assets binding.

> A wrangler.toml file was found but it does not appear to be valid. Did you mean to use wrangler.toml to configure Pages? If so, then make sure the file is valid and contains the `pages_build_output_dir` property. Skipping file and continuing.

```jsonc
{
	"name": "svelte-kit-cloudflare",
	"pages_build_output_dir": ".svelte-kit/cloudflare", // required
	"compatibility_flags": ["nodejs_compat"], // easier to add
	"compatibility_date": "2025-12-12" // easier to add
}
```

Remove trailing comma from the `wrangler.jsonc` example

It does work in Workers but causes parse error in Pages. Related:

- https://github.com/sveltejs/kit/issues/14926#issuecomment-3647462733
- https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/102061

```jsonc
{
	"name": "<any-name-you-want>",
	"main": ".svelte-kit/cloudflare/_worker.js",
	"compatibility_date": "2025-01-01",
	"assets": {
		"binding": "ASSETS",
		"directory": ".svelte-kit/cloudflare", // causes parse error in the Pages build system
	}
}
```

Add the `$schema` field in `wrangler.jsonc`. Requires `wrangler` installation, but it provides type safety.

```jsonc
{
	"$schema": "./node_modules/wrangler/config-schema.json", // Added
```

### Reproduction

https://github.com/hyunbinseo/svelte-kit-cloudflare

### Logs

```

### Describe the bug

I posted this issue already on sveltekit-superforms but it seems to be a Svelte 5 issue. https://github.com/ciscoheat/sveltekit-superforms/issues/509

Apparently in Svelte 5 there is a reactivity problem when you use `bind:` with a list item in a loop it breaks reference.

- What I'm wondering about is that while the $form store is updating correctly and reactively the form param inside the onUpdate callback is not up to date

- This issue seems to only occur in Svelte 5, that's why it seems to me like a bug



``` svelte

<form use:enhance method="post">
	{#each $form.priceRules ?? [] as priceRule}
		<!-- this doesn't work (only when updating another form field afterwards: -->
		<PriceSelection bind:selectedItem={priceRule.priceCategory} />

		<!-- this works: -->
		<!-- <PriceSelection bind:selectedItem={$form.priceRules[0].priceCategory} /> -->
	{/each}

	<button type="submit">submit</button>
</form>
```

sveltejs/svelte#15370.body:
### Describe the bug

The `<svelte:boundary>` do nothing on SSR !

On the [REPL](https://svelte.dev/playground/27336fb2096e495998d33fbb29990b02?version=5.20.2) we can see that code like this :
```svelte
<svelte:boundary>
	template
	{#snippet failed()}
		error
	{/snippet}
</svelte:boundary>
```
Will generate this server code :
```js
	{
		function failed($$payload) {
			$$payload.out += `<!---->error`;
		}

		$$payload.out += `<!---->template`;
	}
```

The template is written, but there no try/catch and the `failed()` snippet is never caller when an error occurs in the template !

Actually this will produce an HTTP 500 error :(

I think it should render the `failed()` snippet instead...

### Reproduction

Click on the links "Bad page" and "Good page" to see that the error is handheld on client-site.
Then, when on the "Bad page", click on the "reload to force SSR" button to regenerate the page via SSR... => HTTP 500

https://www.sveltelab.dev/ravzziihmogkq1a

### Logs

```shell

```

```shell
Done on sveltelabs with last version
```

### Severity

annoyance

Before you could customize a `<select>`, if you put things like an image or SVG into the `<option>` element, the browser would ignore them, but Svelte throws an error:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f969d1dd-7e98-480e-a385-49736d788e77)

With the release of Chrome 135, [the `<select>` element can now be customized using CSS](https://developer.chrome.com/blog/a-customizable-select) and [supports rich HTML content](https://developer.chrome.com/blog/a-customizable-select#a_select_can_now_include_rich_html_content). ### Describe the problem

When trying to type `$props`, a common requirement tends to be providing a type to `children`. To a complete svelte newbie, it might be helpful to let them know that it is a snippet. Would it be pertinent to have a note linking to the [typing snippets](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/snippet#Typing-snippets) section inside the documentation for `$props` for that? Somewhere around/below the [type safety section](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/$props#Type-safety)?

### Describe the proposed solution

Something along the lines of:
> [!NOTE] It is quite common to provide a type for `children`. `children` is typed as a [Snippet](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/snippet), [an section on providing types for Snippets](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/snippet#Typing-snippets) is provided here.

### Importance

nice to have

### Describe the bug

Prior to [this commit](https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/commit/2d2772a4a15fbafcb101f55458ac6486f7853fd2) on June 6th an error thrown in an effect was propagated to the `<svelte:boundary>`. 

```js
// test.svelte
<script lang="ts">
  $effect(() => {
    throw new Error()
  })
</script>

// page.svelte
<svelte:boundary>
  <Test />
  No error caught.
  {#snippet failed()}
    Error caught!
  {/snippet}
</svelte:boundary>

// Previously this would show "Error caught!", but it now show "No error caught".
```

In recent versions, the error is caught and passed to handle_error, but since `(effect.f & EFFECT_RAN) === 0` and the current effect is not a boundary effect, the error is rethrown. This happens even though we are not creating a subtree at the time the error is thrown. 

```js
export function handle_error(error) {
  ...
  if ((effect.f & EFFECT_RAN) === 0) {
    // if the error occurred while creating this subtree, we let it
    // bubble up until it hits a boundary that can handle it
    if ((effect.f & BOUNDARY_EFFECT) === 0) {
      throw error; // <-- WE END UP HERE
    }

    effect.fn(error);
  } else {
    // otherwise we bubble up the effect tree ourselves
    invoke_error_boundary(error, effect);
  }
```

Maybe the condition can be refined, so that the error is passed to `invoke_error_boundary()` instead?

### Reproduction

https://github.com/johannesodland/svelte-boundary-issue

sveltejs/svelte#16343.body:
### Describe the bug

The warning `state_referenced_locally` does not show up consistently where I believe it should.

Consider this example:

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
    // state created from props should warn but doesn't
    let { x } = $props()
    let y = $state(x) // ❌ this should warn but doesn't
    let z = $state(y) // ❌ this should warn but doesn't

    // state created from other state only warns "one level deep"
    let a = $state(0)
    let b = $state(a) // ✅ this does warn
    let c = $state(b) // ❌ this should warn but doesn't

    // state created from implicitly undefined state (putting in `undefined`, `null`, `0`, `false` here does warn)
    let h = $state()
    let j = $state(h) // ❌ this should warn but doesn't
</script>
```

As outlines in the comments above, the warnings are shown very inconsistently.
I believe in many cases (see ❌ above) warnings should be shown.

The main problems are:

- `$state(...)` created from `$props()` does not warn
- `$state(...)` created from other `$state(...)` only warns "one level deep"
- `$state(...)` created from implicitly undefined state (`$state()`) does not warn

Maybe there are more cases that I did not cover that should warn as well.

### Reproduction

https://svelte.dev/playground/62605b146a9e4c4f812b9f872ba21288?version=5.35.5

### Logs

```shell
This reference only captures the initial value of a. Did you mean to reference it inside a closure instead?
https://svelte.dev/e/state_referenced_locally (state_referenced_locally)
```

### System Info

```shell
playground
```

### Severity

annoyance

sveltejs/svelte#16528.body:
https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/$bindable tells:

> Ordinarily, props go one way, from parent to child. This makes it easy to understand how data flows around your app.
>
> In Svelte, component props can be bound, which means that data can also flow up from child to parent. This isn’t something you should do often, but it can simplify your code if used sparingly and carefully.

I think it misses an explanation why it could be done but shouldn't be done often. Or just don't tell anything ?

Actually I see it as a big topic, because I feel that bidirectional binding allows local source of truth and component responsibility, which is the state of art of OOP / SASS / API. I've yet to be convinced why suddenly with UI it's different and the flow should only go one way, because it equals to global variables, which is known to be the enemy of scalability and responsibility.
sveltejs/svelte#16528.title: Explain why `$bindable` shouldn't be often used

sveltejs/svelte#16547.body:
### Describe the problem

I am doing something like this:

```svelte
{#each Object.entries(tabs) as [tabId, tab] (tabId)}
    <TabView selected={tabId === selected} bind:this={tabViews[tabId]}>
        Content
    </TabView>
{/each}
```

Binding directly to an object `const tabViews = {}` via `bind:this={tabViews[tabId]}` does not work when adding more tabs dynamically, then references to existing ("cached" by `#each` because of given key `(tabId)`) tabs are lost. See this result after adding more tabs: `{player: null, group: Proxy(Object), call: Proxy(Object), chat: Proxy(Object)}`. ❗

I then tried binding to a map using getters and setters as described in https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/bind#Function-bindings like so `bind:this={null, (v) => tabViews.set(tabId, v)}`. This failed with a runtime error: `Binding as described in https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/bind#Function-bindings` ❗

Therefore I changed the code to this `bind:this={() => null, (v) => tabViews.set(tabId, v)}` which finally worked ✅

### Describe the proposed solution

# Request

Please add support for the mentioned https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/bind#Function-bindings for `bind:this`.

Or if that is not sensible, then maybe add this behaviour to the docs?

### Importance

nice to have

sveltejs/svelte#16775.body:
### Describe the bug

Ok, just thinking about an appropriate title was difficult.

Here it goes, my attempt to describe this problem.

#### Background

Making an online REPL demo for my router (@wjfe/n-savant), I decided to show a simple way to add transitions.  It works.  However, if the matching route itself continues to match after navigating (in the demo's case, a route parameter changes), then animation doesn't take place because the component is not being destroyed so transitions don't happen.

This is the REPL:  [@wjfe/n-savant demo](https://svelte.dev/playground/d273d356947e48c0822a65402fd06fac)

So I figured:  Ok, if the Svelte tutorial ever taught me anything is that I can trigger transitions using `{#key}`.  So let's add `{#key rp}` (`rp` is a reactive object that holds the matching route's parameter values) to AniRoute.svelte.

#### The Issue

Refer to this other REPL:  [@wjfe/n-savant playground](https://svelte.dev/playground/ed8a63dc8ed74ed4a583718f73549599)

Open it, navigate to either "Big", "Medium" or "Small", then back to "Home".  What you're seeing is the **Home.svelte** component and the **Logos.svelte** component being rendered at the same time.  It is the big logo regardless of your previous choice because the route param that dictates the logo size named "variant" has changed to `undefined`.  This means the big logo.

Logic issue?  Doesn't look like it.  Check the console log:

```plaintext
# These lines appear when you navigate to a logo route.
logo RS from router (update): {match: true, routeParams: {…}}
Variant: 64
Logos mounted.

# These lines appear when you go back home.
logo RS from router (update): {match: false, routeParams: undefined}
Logos unmounted.
```

"Logos unmounted.".  Well, its contents are visible.  Why?

### Reproduction

I don't have a minimal reproducible example, even though I have tried for hours to reproduce.  I just don't know what triggers it, except that it has to do with `{#key}`.

If we remove `{#key}` (AniRoute.svelte, line 21) then everything works just fine.

Maybe this requires a master in Svelte internals to understand.  I haven't been able to figure this one out.  I'll continue trying as time permits.

### Describe the bug

Prior to 5.39.0, awaiting an async function inside a function assigned to a `@const` worked without issue. From 5.39.0 onward, even though console logging shows that both the parent and child functions are getting called with the right values, the actual component doesn't seem to be updating/rerendering, as if the `activeTab` assignment did nothing.

Things that seem to change behavior:
* Moving the `await doSomething()` before the `activeTab = tab.id` fixes it (but in our case, `doSomething` actually relies on `activeTab` being changed first, so this doesn't work for us)

sveltejs/svelte#16934.body:
### Describe the bug

When awaiting `render()` when `compilerOptions.experimental.async` isn't enabled, the `experimental_async_ssr` warning is shown. Problem is, there's no way to know if the result is awaitable from its shape because `then` is always present: https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/blob/main/packages/svelte/src/internal/server/renderer.js#L352-L384. That's an issue for us in Astro.

My recommendation would be to only set the `then` property if `async_mode_flag` is true so we can do checks like `'then' in result`:

```js
let result = render(...)
if (isPromise(result)) {
  result = await result
}
```

That currently forces us to make a pretty big workaround, see https://github.com/withastro/astro/pull/14526.

I'd be happy to open a PR

### Reproduction

See https://github.com/withastro/astro/issues/14508 and https://stackblitz.com/edit/github-tpnsqnas?file=src%2Fpages%2Findex.astro

### Describe the problem

I'm always frustrated when i use `$inspect`. The log shows the location as a svelte util (`inspect.js`) and there is no indication of the true source of the call.

`$inspect` calls should show the file in which they were called, or a truncated stack trace.

sveltejs/svelte#17080.body:
### Describe the problem

Currently, the only thing you can pass to shadow DOM contructor is either [`mode: "open"`](https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/blob/8ebc3b1337bd12931f5608ab608b9668e10aa6c2/packages/svelte/src/internal/client/dom/elements/custom-element.js#L49) or nothing at all.
https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/custom-elements#Component-options

This means that you can't create shadow DOM with `closed` shadow root or change any other options of [`attachShadow()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/attachShadow).



### Describe the proposed solution

Instead of passing only `"none"` to the `shadow` custom element option, allow passing the whole [`options`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element/attachShadow#options) object, which would be then passed to `attachShadow()`.

This would allow changing shadow root mode to `closed` and would also allow you to customize all the other options.

This would probably be considered a breaking change for everyone already using `shadow: "none"`, so you should be able to still pass either:
- `shadow: "none"` or `shadow: "open"` - to maintain backwards compatibility
- something like `shadow: { mode: 'closed', clonable: false }` (or any other options)

### Importance

nice to have
sveltejs/svelte#17080.title: Allow passing custom options to shadow DOM constructor

### Describe the bug

In JavaScript it is possible to spread Symbol keys into rest props:

```js
const S = Symbol('key');
const a = { foo: 'bar', [S]: 42 }
const { foo, ...rest } = a;
console.log(rest[S]); // logs 42
```

But in Svelte this doesn't work:

```svelte
<script>
  const S = Symbol('key');
	
  let a = $state({ foo: 'bar', [S]: 42 });
  $inspect('a[S]:', a[S]); // 42

  let { foo, ...rest } = $derived(a);
  $inspect('rest[S]:', rest[S]); // undefined
</script>
```

### Describe the bug

If you have a value X that causes a pending batch, and while that batch is pending cause creation of another batch which creates a new branch and therefore new template effects which also read that value X, those template effects are not correctly rescheduled once the pending batch resolves. As a result, the shown values of X get out of sync (once shows the old, once the new value). 

I believe it happens because the pending batch does not know of the new effects that are created (they are not in its (maybe_)dirty arrays), therefore does not schedule them as (maybe_)dirty on revival and therefore never runs them.

### Reproduction

https://svelte.dev/playground/c7e416c4b97446468ee85f3abf4bc676?version=5.41.4#H4sIAAAAAAAACn1STYvbMBD9K4O6YJuY2NCb1zEsvfXQFtpb04PjjNNhZUlI47WF0H8vSpbsd4-a9-bNG70JQvUTikbcOa8GOMwnUYqRJDrR_A6CvUlgKojySjVm6x5Qcqodeofv1QetGBU70YjWDZYMd3u1Z5qMtgxf_pI8wmj1BNm2Or8eW7PbRJPIsMIObhz3jHm2aCuPWXHF_BNWp2pbPc1Q7WFm1gq0GiQN97vQn3fLC9h1EJJEVaVNGzjjoBV4OOCoLQLThGBn5WA2t7CQlHCPaGAFUo6OCB56hkc_SSq5zGZFD2gdZnsVY7e21cXB-24uPvxmEzu_2bzghk80pr13zzVjGhP6pScGhQv8sHoih7lNMg75F02oZ85tCZ_rui6Kc0N7-eKwRqi6vQoVjfE6wkMH9X9oohSMK4uG7Yyx_OAOnsf28hJeIR_cQgoywAoxhWmsNi6_JDxo5bTErdSnfH0Tb_j68_u3rWNL6kSjz8Maizee_5SCe5ILqaNoxl46jP8Amw0uj-sCAAA

sveltejs/svelte#17114.body:
### Describe the bug

I have a page where you can contact other users. The route is `/contact/[id]` the +page.server.ts file for that page retrieves the user information you are trying to contact from the DB and returns it via load. Then the svelte head is set on that page to display a title of `Contact <name>` and also displays the info of the person you are contacting. The links to reach these pages are just simple anchor tags.

The issue I am facing is that just hovering over the anchor tag the data is preloaded and updates the page title and any other head content before clicking on the anchor. Also, clicking on the anchor tag ends up causing the app to crash or freeze. Once you reload the page, the previous clicks are added into the window history.

sveltejs/svelte#17176.body: ### Describe the bug In async Svelte versions (`5.36.0+`) there have been some significant performance regressions (`~8x`) when rendering many LayerChart charts. While some subsequent versions have made progress to restore some performance, the more recent releases (`5.40.0+`) have gotten much worse (`~27x`). Below is rendering 20 `BarChart` instances on different Svelte versions: version | average | screenshot | REPL --- | --- | --- | --- `5.35.0` (baseline) | `~600ms` | | [REPL](https://svelte.dev/playground/2b7d66954a79447581e5b3971171497e?version=5.35.0) `5.39.0` | `~5300ms` | | [REPL](https://svelte.dev/playground/2b7d66954a79447581e5b3971171497e?version=5.39.0) `5.39.13` | `~4700ms` | | [REPL](https://svelte.dev/playground/2b7d66954a79447581e5b3971171497e?version=5.39.13) `5.43.8` | `~16500ms` | | [REPL](https://svelte.dev/playground/2b7d66954a79447581e5b3971171497e?version=5.43.8) `5.43.11` | `~850ms` | | [REPL](https://svelte.dev/playground/2b7d66954a79447581e5b3971171497e?version=5.43.11) `5.46.1` | `~890ms` | | [REPL](https://svelte.dev/playground/2b7d66954a79447581e5b3971171497e?version=5.46.1) `5.48.1` | `~800ms` | | [REPL](https://svelte.dev/playground/2b7d66954a79447581e5b3971171497e?version=5.48.1) ### Reproduction See REPL links above, notable the most recent [5.43.8](https://svelte.dev/playground/2b7d66954a79447581e5b3971171497e?version=5.43.8) version. For a more real world example, checking out the WIP LayerChart v2 docs [PR](https://github.com/techniq/layerchart/pull/659), which are PR preview deployed [here](https://techniq-docs-v2.layerchart.pages.dev). A good example is going to any component page such as [BarChart](https://techniq-docs-v2.layerchart.pages.dev/docs/components/BarChart) and clicking "View all" (warning: your browser won't be happy). I've been using `5.39.13` to development which allowed for async/remote functions support and enough performance to be patient with, but `5.40.0+` can crash the browser (but also fixes some deployment issues in older versions). I recently bumped to the latest `5.43.8` to fix some deployment issues and help report issues. ### Logs ```shell ``` ### System Info ```shell svelte `5.36.0+` and `5.40.0+` ``` ### Severity blocking an upgrade

### Describe the bug

sveltejs/svelte#17235.body:
### Describe the problem

Sort of similar to https://github.com/sveltejs/devalue/issues/5 (wow, 2018), if I try to serialize an object for hydration and it contains instances of `Promise` from another context - like it would when I'm running code with `vm.runInNewContext()` - then Svelte fails to see this as a promise, and devalue balks at having to serialize an arbitrary POJO.

### Describe the proposed solution

The only thing I can think of is to try duck typing this. How would we feel about `result && typeof result.then == 'function'` instead of `result instanceof Promise`?

### Importance

nice to have

sveltejs/svelte#17313.body:
### Describe the bug

Hey, thanks for launching the new hydratable feature! We're already taking advantage of it here at Stack Overflow.

We've noticed one problem with the current implementation though. If a promise rejects (eg. through a thrown exception), hydratable reasonably fails to serialize the Error object returned by the promise. However, it then throws an exception outside of the promise chain. This causes the entire node process to crash since I _think_ the exception was thrown in a background thread, not the main thread.

cc @elliott-with-the-longest-name-on-github @Rich-Harris @kaysef

I'm happy to open a PR to help fix this, I just need some guidance on what the intent is on these promise chains below.

## Source of the problem

I think the problematic bit is in the `entry.serialized` assignment:
https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/blob/46603d93cbe072ea463a099894e2bccdd264051e/packages/svelte/src/internal/server/hydratable.js#L58-L88

I think that this accidentally introduces three branches of promise chains, only one of which is actually used:
1. The first promise is defined on line 60 with `const p = value.then((v) => `r(${uneval(v)})`).catch((d) => e.hydratable_serialization_failed(...));`. The promise we gave it throws an exception, so the `uneval` never runs and we jump to the `catch`. However, because `catch` is _returning_ the error object instead of throwing it, the promise is now an accepted promise of type `Promise<Error>`. It is _not_ the expected type of a rejected promise.
2. A second promise is created on line 70 with `p.catch(() => {});`. `p` is an accepted promise, so the catch handler is never called. Notably, this new promise created by `p.catch(...)` is not assigned back to `p`, it's just discarded, so even if it _did_ run, it wouldn't ever transform the promise `p`.
3. A third promise is created on line 74: `p.finally(() => unresolved?.delete(p));`. This refers back to the first promise, not the second promise, because `p` was never reassigned. This third promise is then discarded and not reassigned to `p`. (It's unclear to me if the `finally()` block will ever execute, because this third promise is discarded and not assigned to `p`?)
4. A fourth promise is created on line 81 with `p.then((s) => { entry.serialized = entry.serialized.replace(placeholder, s);})`. This promise refers again to the first promise. `p` is an accepted promise containing an `Error` object, so this new fourth promise is happily (and incorrectly) pushed as a value to `entry.promises`.
5. The data has now been corrupted in `entry.promises` - it contains an `Promise<Error>` object. We probably should have thrown by now and not corrupted `entry.promises`. Presumably, something later tries to do something with that `Error` object and crashes in a background thread, taking the whole node process with it.

### Diagram of promise chains

```
value = new Promise(() => { throw new Error("nope")})
p = value
1+4--> p.then(uneval).catch(hydratable_serialization_failed).then(entry.serialized = ...)
2  --> p.catch(() => {})
3  --> p.finally(() => unresolved?.delete(value))
```


## Possible fix

I think if you did `throw e.hydratable_serialization_failed(...)` inside the first `catch(...)`, it _might_ work? However, the promise chains are still kind of messy - promises 2 and 3 aren't being assigned back to `p` and are thus being discarded (and possibly never having their extra `then(...)`/`catch(...)`/`finally(...)` executed?)

### Reproduction

Here's my minimal repro project. It's a Fastify server that tries to render the Svelte component using a Vite-built SSR file. 
https://github.com/kylejrp/vite-ssr-crash

Steps to reproduce:
```
npm install
npm run dev
```

Then, open http://localhost:3000/. The first request will show an error object, but then the server crashes in the background. Trying to reload the page will time out because the server never responds.

### Failing component

You can reproduce this with the minimal Svelte component:

```
<script lang="ts">
  import { hydratable } from "svelte";
  const foo = await hydratable("foo", () => new Promise(() => { throw new Error("nope")}));
</script>

{foo}
```

This crashes the entire node process on the background thread. This exception is not caught by Fastify's global error handler and the entire server becomes unresponsive. Visiting http://localhost:3000/ will respond with an error message on the first request, but will then crash shortly after on the background thread and make the entire server unresponsive.

### Working component

If you move the promise from being called by hydratable, it instead correctly bubbles up the exception on the main thread and is handled by Fastify's global error handler. You can make repeated requests to http://localhost:3000/ and Fastify responds with a 500 error each time.

```
<script lang="ts">
    const foo = await new Promise(() => { throw new Error("nope")});
</script>

{foo}
```

### Describe the bug

Svelte each block now supports `undefined` and `null`.

- https://github.com/sveltejs/language-tools/pull/2863

The [docs](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/each) should be updated to match this behavior.

Current explanation is incorrect:

> in other words, anything that can be used with `Array.from`.

```js
Array.from(undefined); // Uncaught TypeError: can't access property Symbol.iterator, items is undefined
Array.from(null); // Uncaught TypeError
```

Stating this in the docs will avoid LLMs from writing `?? []` inside the `each` loop.

### Describe the bug

Upgrading svelte from `5.43.14` to `5.44.0` causes async rendering to fail, throwing the `server_context_required` error. We still call `render()` in what seems an okay way so I'm not sure what we should do about: https://github.com/withastro/astro/blob/main/packages/integrations/svelte/src/server.ts#L58.

### Reproduction

- Clone https://github.com/withastro/astro/
- Checkout to `fix/svelte-server-context-required`
- `pnpm i --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm build`
- `node --test packages/integrations/svelte/test/async-rendering.test.js`

sveltejs/svelte#17394.body: ### Describe the bug Hi! I encountered a very weird bug that causes the page to hang () ```svelte {#each Array.from( { length: numberOfDays }, () => Temporal.PlainDate.from(start), ) as day} {await Promise.resolve(day.toString())} {/each} ``` When clicking the button, the page hangs However, when replacing `numberOfDays` by its numerical value (`1`), the bug no longer happens ```svelte {#each Array.from({ length: 1 }, () => Temporal.PlainDate.from(start)) as day} {await Promise.resolve(day.toString())} {/each} ``` The bug does not reproduce in the REPL, however, something else happens: when the variable is inlined, the code is no longer reactive. ### Reproduction - Original code https://svelte.dev/playground/067a10bdddc843b2b03d9a1ce3344434?version=5.46.0 Works in the REPL, hangs in real-world SvelteKit application The `{await Promise.resolve(day.toString())}` gets transpiled into: ```js $.template_effect(($0) => $.set_text(text, $0), void 0, [() => Promise.resolve($.get(day).toString())]); ``` - Patched code https://svelte.dev/playground/a34afe48ce5c4f57b8e82d196dc6ddc9?version=5.46.0 No longer reactive in the REPL, does not hang in real-world SvelteKit application The `{await Promise.resolve(day.toString())}` gets transpiled into: ```js $.template_effect(($0) => $.set_text(text, $0), void 0, [() => Promise.resolve(day.toString())]); ``` Fixing this issue might not fix my original bug, but at least it's something that can be reproduced in the REPL ### Logs ```shell ``` ### System Info ```shell Svelte 5.46.0 ``` ### Severity annoyance

sveltejs/svelte#17418.body:
Closes #16540
Follow up to https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/commit/a86e52b17466ff8c5bd6268932da6954d08a87d6

This PR adds support for using Svelte in XHTML and XML documents by:

1. Producing XML-compliant output for boolean attributes (e.g., `required` becomes `required=""`)
2. Using proper namespace checks for uses of `tagName` / `nodeName`, as well as ensuring that the values have the correct case.
3. Replaces use of `document.createElement` with `document.createElementNS`, defaulting to the HTML namespace. This ensures that even documents loaded in pure XML mode (e.g., `index.xml`, as opposed to `index.xhtml`) will work correctly.

### Describe the bug

I looked recenty at the [docs](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/svelte-element) and the [tutorial](https://svelte.dev/tutorial/svelte/svelte-element) which states: 

>The `this` value can be any string, or a falsy value — if it’s falsy, no element is rendered.

When trying to use `svelte-element` `this` attribute to hide an value based on if it is a boolean or not I get the error `name.toLowerCase() is not a function` from vite. I do not know what caused this but since it's from [here](https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/blob/2eb54f57ae9b2411a40f10266133356ab647d907/packages/svelte/src/utils.js#L40), I assume that svelte is responsible for this error.

		showYear: boolean;
		showMonth: boolean;
		headerNameLength: number;
		includeSwitchMonth: boolean;
	};
	type Props = {
		options?: Partial<Options>;

	const defaultOption: Options = {
		showYear: true,

		headerNameLength: 2,
		includeSwitchMonth: true,
	};

	let { options = defaultOption }: Props = $props();

	function option<K extends keyof Options>(option: K): Options[K] {
		return options[option] ?? defaultOption[option];

</script>

sveltejs/svelte#17529.body:
### Describe the bug

In Svelte 5.48.1, using `await` inside `$derived()` with `experimental.async: true` throws the error:

```
TypeError: $.wait is not a function. (In '$.wait([$$promises[20], $$promises[20]])', '$.wait' is undefined)
```

### Cause

PR #17461 added the `wait` function to `packages/svelte/src/internal/client/reactivity/async.js` and modified the compiler to generate `$.wait()` calls:

```diff
+			run.thunks.push(b.thunk(b.call('$.wait', b.array(blockers))));
```

However, the `wait` function was **never exported from `packages/svelte/src/internal/client/index.js`**.

The function exists at line 300 of `async.js`:
```js
export function wait(blockers) {
	return Promise.all(blockers.map((b) => b.promise));
}
```

But the exports from `async.js` in `index.js` are only:
```js
export {
	for_await_track_reactivity_loss,
	run,
	save,
	track_reactivity_loss,
	run_after_blockers
} from './reactivity/async.js';
```

### Reproduction

```svelte
<script>
  // svelte.config.js has compilerOptions.experimental.async: true
  
  async function getData() {
    return { name: 'test' };
  }
  
  const data = $derived(await getData());
</script>

{data.name}
```

### Expected behavior

The `wait` function should be exported from `index.js`:

```diff
export {
	for_await_track_reactivity_loss,
	run,
	save,
	track_reactivity_loss,
-	run_after_blockers
+	run_after_blockers,
+	wait
} from './reactivity/async.js';
```

### Workaround

Downgrade to Svelte 5.48.0 which does not have this issue.

### System Info

```
Svelte: 5.48.1
```

### Severity

blocking an upgrade

sveltejs/svelte#17570.body:
### Describe the bug

While the [client implementation of `createContext`](https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/blob/06e6ef83b801a3ca014af4629abad76ac2d35eef/packages/svelte/src/internal/client/context.js#L81) throws `e.missing_context()` if the context hasn't been set, the [server implementation](https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/blob/06e6ef83b801a3ca014af4629abad76ac2d35eef/packages/svelte/src/internal/server/context.js#L20) returns the result of of `getContext` directly, so it returns undefined rather than throwing if the context hasn't been set.

This can lead to subtle bugs, for example catching the error to provide a default value works on the client but silently fails on the server. The `get()` type signature is also wrong for server code where undefined can sneak past the types.

I hit this using optional context to manage heading levels. The server rendered different tags than the client, making for a funky heading tree and hydration mismatches.

Making both throw aligns with [documented behaviour](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/svelte#createContext). But would making a function start throwing count as a breaking change? I'm happy to pitch in with a PR once I know what approach to take.

```svelte
<!-- Parent.svelte -->
<script>
  // This async derived in parent triggers the bug
  const something = $derived(await Promise.resolve('test'))
  
  let active = $state('some-id')
</script>

{#if active}
  <Child id={active} />
{/if}

  // Logs 'undefined' on unmount - BUG
  const data = $derived(await Promise.resolve(id).then(x => {
    console.log('promise resolved with:', x)
    return x
  }))
</script>

```shell

sveltejs/svelte#17660.body:
### Describe the problem

## 1

> An exception is that independent `$derived` expressions will update independently, even though they will run sequentially when they are first created:

```js
// these will run sequentially the first time,
// but will update independently
let a = $derived(await one());
let b = $derived(await two());
```

### 1-1

'run sequentially' is confusing.

For example, the promises can be created sequentially, but `b` can resolve first. The truth is:

> ...the second `$derived` will not be created until the first one has resolved.

Why not be specific like the quote in [`await_waterfall` docs](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/runtime-warnings#Client-warnings-await_waterfall)?

Clarification is needed because `$derived` isn't vanilla JavaScript.

```js
// this is intuitive. `two` is called after `one` is resolved.
await one();
await two();

// this is not intuitive
$derived(await one());
$derived(await two());
```

### 1-2

'update independently' is confusing.

Can `one` and `two` rerun? They don't have any dependencies.

```js
$derived(await one());
$derived(await two());
```

Example from query remote function seems better:

```js
let a = $derived(await one());
const post = $derived(await getPost(params.slug)); // params.slug is the dependency
```

## 2

> Anything read synchronously inside the `$derived` expression (or `$derived.by` function body) is considered a _dependency_ of the derived state.

Claude is complaining that `params.slug` is not read synchronously, thus not a dependency.

```js
$derived(await getPost(params.slug));
```

What are considered dependencies in async Svelte and `$derived`?

## Note

I believe I've used `await` inside `$derived.by` as an alternative to `untrack`. Maybe this should be warned on Svelte 5 to 6 migration? I didn't see it in the breaking changes section.

### Describe the proposed solution

See above

### Importance

nice to have
sveltejs/svelte#17660.title: docs: clarify `$derived` `await` examples, dependency tracking

### Describe the bug

If a component throws while being mounted, that would normally be fine because the developer could fix the code and Svelte would remount the component using HMR. But now, even without having async enabled, HMR fails to remount the component. I've bisected this down to #15844. And found a potential fix which I've made a PR for.

### Reproduction

The following reproductions are the same except for the version of Svelte and have a line of code that can be toggled on or off to have the component throw when it mounts.

latest (5.50.1): https://www.sveltelab.dev/32149g118o5egv7
pre-async (5.35.7): https://www.sveltelab.dev/3032460y2gelaoq

Reproduction steps:
1. Mount a component that has been compiled using the `hmr` compiler option.
2. Hot reload the component but have the component throw an exception on mount.
3. Hot reload the component but without an exception during mount.

(this excludes the playground since it doesn't use `hmr: true`)

### Logs

```shell

```

```shell
n/a
```

sveltejs/svelte#17694.body:
### Describe the bug

In the `mount` function, if you pass the same `target` twice, it could cause event handling to completely break. An example of this is portals. If your app is mounted under `body` and a portal is also mounted under `body`, you can have `mount` called with the same `target` twice.

### Reproduction

https://svelte.dev/playground/948e793ddfe443f68548b22cec26789a?version=5.50.3

- Right-click the button. The context menu is shown correctly.
- Click the button to show the dialog, then click anywhere to close it, unmounting the portal.
- Right-clicking the button no longer works.

For some odd reason, this behavior can only be observed when a `contextmenu` event handler is added to the trigger using Svelte's `on` utility, causing the `oncontextmenu` handler internal to bits-ui to never receive the `contextmenu` event.

sveltejs/svelte#17721.body:
### Describe the bug

https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/each#Keyed-each-blocks

> The key can be any object

So I tried using array as a key for #each block:

```
{#each things as thing ([thing.group, thing.id])}
```

Works fine when page loads, but when data changes, it throws error.

Had to convert to string in order to get it working:

```
{#each things as thing ([thing.group, thing.id].join('-')}
```

So either docs need updating, or #each needs fixing to support array keys

### Reproduction

https://svelte.dev/playground/1db5362a3e974b209b11ae389758c93f?version=5.51.2

Click button, check browser console.

sveltejs/svelte#17758.body:
### Describe the bug

The [documentation](https://svelte.dev/docs/svelte/basic-markup#:~:text=all%20others%20are-,coerced%20to%20strings,-.) says,

> A JavaScript expression can be included as text by surrounding it with curly braces.
>```
>{expression}
>```
>Expressions that are null or undefined will be omitted; all others are [coerced to strings](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/String#string_coercion).

Placing an expression in curly braces sometimes produces code that embeds the expression in a template string, which correctly coerces it to a string first:
```js
$.set_text(text, `${value ?? ''} `);
```
However, other times code is produced that passes the expression directly to `set_text`:
```js
$.set_text(text_1, value);
```
and `set_text` then [concatenates the value with `+ ''`](https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/blob/0c7f815143c878d4c2819e45e5f2992da1209f63/packages/svelte/src/internal/client/render.js#L48), which actually coerces it to a primitive (by first trying `valueOf`), not a string.

This has observable, real-world effects. For example, Temporal types support `toString`, but [throw on `valueOf`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Temporal/Duration/valueOf#exceptions).

#15287 seems related to determining which form to emit. Because it's effectively predicated on a compiler heuristic, it's tricky as an end-user to work out why correct-looking code sometimes works and sometimes doesn't. I've shown the differences in the playground link below.

However, I suspect all that's needed is to change the coercion method used by `set_text` (maybe to just use a template string there as well), rather than change anything about the emitted code.

### Reproduction

[Playground link](https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.53.0#H4sIAAAAAAAAE3WOMU-EQBCF_8o4NpCA5NSKQxJjY2dhYeFarOxw2WQdNuzcoSH8d7McnDmjzRTf-17ejMj6g7DER3Kug6HrnYGEjBUyKWbYWkcBy9cR5ctHLwLM1ta991fhQE4ie9eB_uJNx0IsAUusQtNbL7ViJY3TIcCLNTsSGCNRIt2z9JZ3SQoj9CT7nkHhShXCdPQO2u3pqT3XFrha8cSZjoPAnMEdMA3LZJJuFVfFz0NcXeQ5NDoQbCDPa8Xj3JrOs-tjVvl6iavC_6rfnJQH7RyZEv5Vb5elS9vCZn77NDoWtp0UY4ZCn4Kl9Hua3jIUbd1g2WDZahdo-gY-NO1lwwEAAA)

sveltejs/svelte#17785.body:
### Describe the bug

Svelte's scope tracker reuses the same scope for a function's arguments and body. Body declarations end up referenced in argument initializers shadowing the parent scopes' declarations, potentially making an initializer [skip rune transformation logic](https://svelte.dev/playground/590c171f1436486e9072bbc2641c9b53?version=5.53.3).

~~I tried fixing this a few different ways, but I couldn't find a clean way to deal with this due to my lack of familiarity with the v5 codebase (hi, everyone, miss you!). My thinking was to create two separate scopes, one for arguments and one for the body (either through the body's `BlockStatement` or the `*Function*` themselves), and perhaps call `add_state_transformers` on the function visitors, but every variation I tried ended up interacting badly with different compilation pipeline assumptions.~~

Turns out I just forgot to make the `BlockStatement` scope non-porous as opposed to the function's. Not sure this is the "cleanest" solution, but `BlockStatement` already had special-casing for the function cases. See attached PR.

### Reproduction

Scope confusion causes runes' objects to be used instead of its values: https://svelte.dev/playground/590c171f1436486e9072bbc2641c9b53?version=5.53.3

Without required transformations, Svelte's incorrect assumption doesn't affect actual output: https://svelte.dev/playground/20b746af78ca4336b0a47c9e60aed1ec?version=5.53.3

```shell

```

sveltejs/svelte#17821.body:
### Describe the bug

When using a `$store` variable inside the text content of an `<option>` element within a `<select>`, the SSR compiler does not wrap the reference with `store_get()`. This causes a `ReferenceError: $store is not defined` during server-side rendering.

This is a common pattern when using i18n libraries like `sveltekit-i18n`, where `t` is a store containing a translation function.

### Reproduction

```svelte
<script>
	import { readable } from 'svelte/store';

	const t = readable((key: string) => key);

	let value = $state('');
</script>

<select bind:value>
	<option disabled value="">{$t('placeholder')}</option>
	<option value="a">A</option>
	<option value="b">B</option>
</select>
```

When this component is server-side rendered, it throws:

```
ReferenceError: $t is not defined
```

All other template expressions correctly compile `$store` to `store_get($$store_subs ??= {}, "$store", store)(...)`, but the `Renderer.option()` code path outputs raw `$store(...)`.

**Compiled SSR output (incorrect):**

```js
$$renderer.option({ disabled: true }, $t('placeholder'));
```

**Expected SSR output:**

```js
$$renderer.option({ disabled: true }, store_get(($$store_subs ??= {}), '$t', t)('placeholder'));
```

### Expected behaviour

`$store` inside `<option>` text content should be compiled with `store_get()` like all other template expressions, and SSR should render without errors.

### Workaround

Extract the store value into a `$derived` variable and use that inside `<option>`:

```svelte
<script>
	import { readable } from 'svelte/store';

	const t = readable((key: string) => key);
	const placeholder = $derived($t('placeholder'));

	let value = $state('');
</script>

<select bind:value>
	<option disabled value="">{placeholder}</option>
	<option value="a">A</option>
	<option value="b">B</option>
</select>
```

### Logs

```shell
sample


ReferenceError: $t is not defined
    at Renderer.child (node_modules/svelte/src/internal/server/renderer.js:214:18)
    at Renderer.select (node_modules/svelte/src/internal/server/renderer.js:337:8)
    at Module.await_block (node_modules/svelte/src/internal/server/index.js:422:3)
```

### System Info

```shell
- Svelte version: 5.53.5
- Related: #16026 (similar issue with `bind:value` on `<select>` with stores, fixed in #16028 — but this is about text content inside `<option>`)


OS: Linux
Node: v24
Package manager: npm
```

sveltejs/svelte#17841.body:
### Describe the bug

Honestly, the code explains the edge-case better than words:

```svelte
<script>	
	let index = $state(0);

	function fn(id) {
		// When index changes to from 0 to 1, we explicitly simulate the undefined error
		if (id === 2) throw new Error("Simulated TypeError");
	}
</script>

<button onclick={() => index = 1}>Trigger</button>

<svelte:boundary>
	{#snippet pending()}Loading...{/snippet}
	{#snippet failed(error)}Error Caught: {error.message}{/snippet}

	{#each [[1], [2]][index] as id (id)}
		{@const result = await fn(id)}
	{/each}
</svelte:boundary>
```

The `result` isn't even used, and `fn` isn't actually doing any async work, but the console will still log this cryptic error:  `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'e')` instead of the real error that was actually thrown.

It requires the error boundary to log (not 100% if that's just in the playground environment) and behaviour does not change by removing the pending snippet.

Also, I'm pretty sure that a `$state` mutation IS required for this to happen.

sveltejs/svelte#17891.body:
### Describe the bug

A store getter that lazily writes `$state` on first read deadlocks the scheduler when read inside a template binding during the flush/commit phase. The UI freezes permanently — no errors, no stack overflow, no crash.

**Works in:** 5.53.7  
**Broken in:** 5.53.8, 5.53.9  
**Likely cause:** #17805 "simplify scheduling logic"

### Reproduction

https://github.com/vdg/svelte-5.53.8-scheduler-deadlock

```bash
git clone https://github.com/vdg/svelte-5.53.8-scheduler-deadlock
cd svelte-5.53.8-scheduler-deadlock
npm install
npm run dev
```

1. Click **"Open panel"**
2. Click **"Counter"** — counter does not update, UI is frozen
3. No console errors

Change to `svelte@5.53.7` → works fine.

### The pattern

A module store with a getter that writes `$state` on first read:

```js
let panelWidth = $state(null)

export default {
  get panelWidth () {
    if (panelWidth === null) panelWidth = DEFAULT_WIDTH  // writes $state
    return panelWidth
  }
}
```

Read by a template binding:

```svelte
<div style:width={store.panelWidth ? `${store.panelWidth}px` : null}>
```

### What happens

1. User action triggers a flush
2. During commit, the `style:width` binding reads `store.panelWidth` for the first time
3. The getter writes `$state` (lazy init)
4. In 5.53.8+, this stalls the scheduler — no further updates are processed

### Workaround

Avoid writing `$state` inside getters:

```js
get panelWidth () {
  return panelWidth ?? DEFAULT_WIDTH  // no state write
}
```

### Severity

Silent — no errors or warnings. The page appears frozen but JS is still running. Very hard to diagnose without knowing to look for this specific pattern.
sveltejs/svelte#17891.title: $state mutation inside getter read during flush deadlocks scheduler (regression in 5.53.8)

sveltejs/svelte#17899.body:
### Describe the bug

After updating to Svelte `5.53.9` and SvelteKit `2.53.4`, running a production build (`vite build`) produces Rollup circular dependency warnings regarding `untrack` and `tick` being re-exported through `index-client.js`.

This occurs with a vanilla Vite configuration (no custom `rollupOptions` or manual chunking enabled). It appears to be a regression or continuation of the recently closed issue **#15973**, but specifically affecting these client-side exports.

**Temporary Workaround Used:**
For now, I had to bypass the warning and ensure stable execution order by manually pinning the internal Svelte modules into a single chunk via `vite.config.js`:
`manualChunks: (id) => { if (id.includes('svelte/src/internal')) return 'svelte-internal'; }`

### Reproduction

I do not have a minimal reproduction repo at the moment, but this triggers on a standard Vite build without any custom chunking configuration. 

Here is the active `vite.config.js` being used when the warning occurs:

```javascript
import { sveltekit } from "@sveltejs/kit/vite"
import { defineConfig } from "vite"

export default defineConfig({
    plugins: [
        sveltekit(),
    ],
    build: {
        target: ["chrome112", "edge112", "safari17"], 
    },
    resolve: {
        alias: {
            $log: "/src/lib/utils/log/log-enabled.js"
        },
    }
})

### Logs

```shell
Export "untrack" of module "node_modules/svelte/src/internal/client/runtime.js" was reexported through module "node_modules/svelte/src/index-client.js" while both modules are dependencies of each other and will end up in different chunks by current Rollup settings. This scenario is not well supported at the moment as it will produce a circular dependency between chunks and will likely lead to broken execution order.
Either change the import in "node_modules/svelte/src/store/utils.js" to point directly to the exporting module or reconfigure "output.manualChunks" to ensure these modules end up in the same chunk.

Export "tick" of module "node_modules/svelte/src/internal/client/runtime.js" was reexported through module "node_modules/svelte/src/index-client.js" while both modules are dependencies of each other and will end up in different chunks by current Rollup settings. This scenario is not well supported at the moment as it will produce a circular dependency between chunks and will likely lead to broken execution order.
Either change the import in ".svelte-kit/generated/root.svelte" to point directly to the exporting module or reconfigure "output.manualChunks" to ensure these modules end up in the same chunk.
```

### Describe the bug

||= evaluates the right-hand-side when the left-hand-side is truthy in Svelte. This is incorrect behavior, which does not occur in vanilla JavaScript.

### Reproduction

https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.53.10#H4sIAAAAAAAACnWRwW7CMBBEf2W1F4MUNfeIIFWVKnHpDxAOxtkUC2cd2RsoCvn3ygpQWujFh-eZ1czugKxbwgIxw8Y6ilisB5RTl1gCmF0Vr133Eg_kJLGtjvSMG89CLBELXEQTbCfLiitpejZiPUPtj-y8rt-to1kf3ByG9F9JnsOqAdnZCA_iCBpSFmiCb0F2BJHCgUJ2s1q5E7e9E9s5ArEtRdBcQ6OtiyAejDY7mmzGc_SOXpz_nKmrW2WQUk2KQNIHBnUppRId05OsMs2CEobxacVV80FUUz27dZxsaW1QgtrTSd3z1PDtsj4op-nrJN7A-Vz-3pzqg1Pzxx73M-a3uI-J_qOL_OdovNj2Ip7Bs3HW7Mvhr2NcCkVZ5JNuWTFmKPQlWEjoadxkKNq6o-Uai0a7SOM3ExrJRm8CAAA

```svelte
<script>
	function downloadFile(url) {
		// If this function downloads a file from the server,
		// it downloads multiple times and fails to cache
		console.log('download', url)
		return 'content'
	}
	const cache = {}
	function downloadIfNeeded() {
		const name = 'key'
		const fileContents = cache[name] ||= downloadFile('url')
		console.log(fileContents)
	}
	downloadIfNeeded()
	downloadIfNeeded()
</script>

<button onclick={downloadIfNeeded}>test</button>
```

### Logs

This is the initial output in Svelte. Clicking the button logs both the "download url" and "content" entries.

```
download url
content
download url
content
```

If you run the `<script>` body in a vanilla JS console, the output is as expected:

```
download url
content
content
```

Interestingly, splitting the statement into two statements results in the correct behavior:

https://svelte.dev/playground/hello-world?version=5.53.10#H4sIAAAAAAAACnWRwWrDMBBEf2XRRQmY-m7sQCkUcukPxDko8qoRkVdGWicNjv-9CCep26QXH55nlpnRIEi1KAohMmGswyiKzSD43CWWgMhuiteue4lHdJzYTkV8xrUnRuIoClFGHWzHq5pqNj1ptp6g8SdyXjXv1uGiD24JQ_pfc57D2gDvbYQHcQQFKQuY4FvgPULEcMSQ3a2WZ-K2d2w7h8C2xQiKGjDKugjsQSu9x8mmPUXv8MX5z4W8uWUGKdWkCMh9IJDXUjLRMX2SladbUMEwPq24Nh-IDTaLe8fJlmaDCuQBz_LK06FN4lu4XKrfI8k-OLmcH0hTvF13hgpm7sdic-3ynv8x4n-0zH9ekcpdz-wJPGln9aEa_jrGFWPkMp90q5pEJhi_WBQcehy3mWBl3clSIwqjXMTxGzGkWHmAAgAA

```js

	const name = 'key'
	cache[name] ||= downloadFile('url')
	const fileContents = cache[name]
	console.log(fileContents)
}
```

sveltejs/svelte#17907.body:
### Describe the bug `$state` reactivity is completely broken when used with `bind:value` on a plain ``. The bound variable updates in JavaScript (verified via `console.log`), but the template never re-renders. **Works in:** 5.53.6 **Broken in:** 5.53.7+ (likely #17805 "simplify scheduling logic" or #17837) We verified this in CI: pinning `svelte@5.53.6` makes our E2E tests pass. Bumping to `5.53.10` breaks them. Same code, same CI environment, only difference is the Svelte version. ### Reproduction The simplest possible Svelte 5 code fails: ```svelte

Debug Test

Value: "{value}"

``` **Expected:** Typing in the input updates the `

` tag in real time. **Actual:** The `

` tag stays as `Value: ""` permanently. The input accepts typing (DOM updates), but the template never re-renders. ### What we tested We progressively simplified to isolate the issue: 1. **Started with:** shadcn-svelte `` wrapper (`$bindable()` + `bind:value` + `{...restProps}`) with `oninput` handler → broken 2. **Removed wrapper:** Plain `` with `value={state}` + `oninput` handler → broken 3. **Removed `oninput`:** Plain `` with just `bind:value={state}` → broken 4. **Removed controller:** Local `$state('')` in the component → broken 5. **Isolated page:** Fresh route with nothing but the 6-line reproduction above → broken At each step, `console.log` confirms the JavaScript value IS being updated. The template simply doesn't re-render. ### Environment - SvelteKit 2.53.4 - Svelte 5.53.10 (broken) / 5.53.6 (works) - Tested in both dev mode and production build (E2E tests use `bun run build && bun run preview`) ### Impact This breaks all reactive input binding — the most fundamental Svelte feature. Any app using `bind:value` on an input with `$state` is affected. Our E2E tests (Playwright) fail because search inputs no longer trigger reactive updates. The `oninput` handlers fire, state is updated in JS, but dependent template blocks (`{#if}`, `{#each}`, text interpolation) never re-render. ### CI evidence - `svelte@5.53.6` → [E2E passes](https://github.com/sannagroup/web-app/commit/0d6b0f66) ✅ - `svelte@5.53.10` → [E2E fails](https://github.com/sannagroup/web-app/commit/bd577619) ❌ Same branch, same code, only the Svelte version differs. ### Severity **Critical** — silent data loss. No errors, no warnings. The input appears to work (you can type), but the reactive state never propagates to the template. Impossible to diagnose without knowing to check the Svelte version.
sveltejs/svelte#17907.title: bind:value on plain <input> with $state stops updating template (regression in 5.53.7+)

### Describe the bug

I'm opening this issue based on the comment in https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/issues/17907#issuecomment-4046751528

Basically, after updating from `5.53.7` to any of the newer versions, my entire application becomes unresponsive.

### Describe the bug

I updated my svelte project to 5.54.1 and encounterend an bug where, when I navigate by using `goto`, my old page is still shown above the page I navigated to. When I reload the page, it only shows the page I navigated to as expected.

I was able to pin down the behavior change to version ` 5.53.13` . With ` 5.53.12` it still works as expected.
While creating the reproductive example I learned that the bug only appears if the new page I navigate to has a new layout.

One possible workaround is to wrap the ` @render children`  in the root layout with ` {#key page.url.pathname}` 

### Reproduction

I used the svelte cli tool to create a new repo, updated the svelte version to the latest available and created a page structure with a root layout, a simple page navigating to an other page which has a new layout definition. You can find this reproduction repo here: https://github.com/Nicklas2751/svelte-goto-navigation-bug

The original repo where I first encountered this bug is open source too, you can find it here: https://github.com/Nicklas2751/LibreHousehold

sveltejs/svelte#17992.body:
### Describe the bug

A `{@const}` inside `{#each}` does not invalidate when its reactive dependency
arrives via a **prop** and that dependency is only referenced inside an array
callback (`.find()`, `.filter()`, `.some()`, etc.) rather than in the outer
expression.

From the template's perspective the prop changed, a sibling `{@const}` that
reads the prop directly updates correctly, but the `{@const}` whose expression
only touches the prop inside a callback closure stays frozen at its previous
value.

## Required conditions

Both must be present:

1. The reactive dependency is a **prop** — does not reproduce when the same
   value is a local variable or a store.
2. That prop value appears **only inside the callback** of the array method —
   removing it from inside and placing it in the outer expression fixes the
   staleness.

No transition, no `{#if}` toggle, no async wrapper needed.


### Reproduction

Link to REPL
https://svelte.dev/playground/9e909f44f22a406a80a639c6ecd69496?version=latest

The playground shows both the buggy and the fixed pattern side by side, driven
by the same prop. Click any button — `selectedItemId` updates correctly in both
sections, but only the **Fixed** section reflects the change in the derived
`selectedItem`.

## Expected behavior

Clicking any button updates `selectedItemId`. Both sections should then resolve
`selectedItem` and show the correct seat count.

## Actual behavior

`selectedItemId` updates in both sections. The **Bug** section never shows
seats — `selectedItem` stays `undefined` on every click. The **Fixed** section
works correctly.

## Workaround

Make the prop-derived scalar appear in the outer expression before the callback:

```svelte
<!-- Buggy: selectedItemId hidden inside callback -->
{@const selectedItem = group.items.find((item) => item.id === selectedItemId)}

<!-- Fixed: selectedItemId visible in outer expression -->
{@const selectedItem = selectedItemId && group.items.find((item) => item.id === selectedItemId)}
```

This forces Svelte to track `selectedItemId` as a direct dependency of
`selectedItem` rather than encountering it only inside the closure.

### Describe the bug

If a component contains typescript like:

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
class X extends Y<{ example: true }> { 
   ...
}
</script>
```

the angle brackets (`<>`) will not be removed, resulting in unexpected token errors.

This used to work in ~2 month old projects, but a fresh setup this week now has the issue.

### Reproduction

https://github.com/pmalacho-mit/svelte-parser-error/tree/main

I also added a working branch, that does not have the error: https://github.com/pmalacho-mit/svelte-parser-error/tree/working

diff: https://github.com/pmalacho-mit/svelte-parser-error/pull/1

Interestingly, I had to fully copy over the package-lock file of a working project -- pinning the exact versions of packages in my package.json was NOT enough.

In the tutorial and repl/playground if you write code which throws an error the entire reactivity for that playground session breaks. Refreshing the output using the refresh button on the tutorial repls does not fix it.

Both in chrome and firefox.  
Playground is running  5.51.2 as shown by the console log message "running Svelte compiler version 5.51.2"

Reproduction:

1. Go on playground or a playground in the tutorial 
2. Write a simple reactive button
3. Write a "bad" statement so it shows an error
4. Delete the bad statement
5. button reactivity is broken even when clicking the refresh button top left of the output.
